LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT VICENTE NAVARRO'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND - THE COURT CONTINUES THE HEARING BASED ON THE DOCUMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF ON SEPTEMBER 13, 2013 ENTITLED "REQUEST FOR DEFENDANT'S MATTER OFF CALENDAR." PLAINTIFF REPRESENTS HIMSELF AND STATES HE IS UNAVAILABLE UNTIL OCTOBER 30, 2013. THE HEARING WILL BE HELD ON OCTOBER 30, 2013 AT 9:30 A.M. ANY OPPOSITION AND REPLY BRIEFS MUST BE FILED AND SERVED IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1005(B). IN LIGHT OF PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTED CONTINUANCE OF THIS HEARING, THE COURT ORDERS THAT PLAINTIFF SHALL NOT EXECUTE ON THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT UNTIL THE COURT RULES ON DEFENDANT'S PENDING MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT JUDGMENT. FURTHER, PLAINTIFF IS DIRECTED TO SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 8.2 B.1. REGARDING COMMUNICATIONS WITH THE COURT REGARDING CONTINUANCES. THAT RULE PROVIDES IN PART THAT "IF THE REQUEST IS TO CONTINUE THE MOTION TO A LATER DATE, THE REQUEST MUST STATE THAT ALL PARTIES AFFECTED BY THE MOTION HAVE AGREED TO THE NEW DATE." OTHERWISE, A PARTY MUST MAKE A MOTION OR AN EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR A CONTINUANCE, AND PROVIDE NOTICE TO THE OTHER PARTIES. SEE CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT 3.1200-3.1207 REGARDING THE REQUIREMENTS FOR EX PARTE APPLICATIONS, INCLUDING GIVING NOTICE TO THE OTHER SIDE. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

calendar.matters:
MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Nov-07-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - JIANJUN XIE, TEL. NO. (510) 251-1935, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PROPER WITH THE HELP OF A CHINESE INTERPRETER, BANTAO SU;
ATTY. MORGAN WEST HANSEN OF TOSCHI, SIDRAN & DOYLE, TEL. NO. (510) 835-3400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT VICENTE NAVARRO's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT, TO DISMISS PLAINTIFF'S CASE WITH PREJUDICE is granted. Defendant's motion to dismiss is granted. CCP 473(d) provides that a void judgment may be set aside at any time. A judgment may be void due to improper service of summons or entry of default without proper service of a statement of damages. (Sakaguchi v. Sakaguchi (2009) 173 Cal.App.4th 852, 858.) Service was not properly effectuated and Defendant did not receive actual notice of the action. In order to properly effectuate substitute service, the complaint and summons must be left with a competent member of the household. (CCP 415.20.) Plaintiff's declaration of service states that the papers were nailed to the door, not left with a member of the household. Additionally, Plaintiff did not properly serve Defendant with a statement of damages pursuant to CCP 425.11(d)(1), which requires that a statement of damages be served on a defendant who has not yet appeared in the same manner as a summons. Mailing the statement is insufficient service. The court grants Defendant's motion to dismiss pursuant to CCP 583.420, as Defendant has not been served within two years of the commencement of the action. Defendant has met his burden in showing that Plaintiff was not diligent in prosecuting the action. Defendant will be unduly prejudiced in his defense of this matter over four years after the subject incident. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

is continued to November 7, 2013 to give Jianjie Xie the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Jianjie Xie must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than October 30, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR # 12998 =(302/MJM)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Sep-16-2013 Continued To Oct-30-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT VICENTE NAVARRO's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND - The Court continues the hearing based on the document filed by Plaintiff on September 13, 2013 entitled "Request for Defendant's Matter Off Calendar." Plaintiff represents himself and states he is unavailable until October 30, 2013. The hearing will be held on October 30, 2013 at 9:30 a.m. Any opposition and reply briefs must be filed and served in accordance with the Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b). In light of Plaintiff's requested continuance of this hearing, the Court orders that Plaintiff shall NOT execute on the default judgment until the Court rules on Defendant's pending Motion to Set Aside Default Judgment. Further, Plaintiff is directed to San Francisco Local Rule 8.2 B.1. regarding communications with the Court regarding continuances. That rule provides in part that "If the request is to continue the motion to a later date, the request must state that all parties affected by the motion have agreed to the new date." Otherwise, a party must make a motion or an ex parte application for a continuance, and provide notice to the other parties. See California Rules of Court 3.1200-3.1207 regarding the requirements for ex parte applications, including giving notice to the other side. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Off Calendar Jun-06-2013 Off Calendar

Ntc Of Default Judgment Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar May-16-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Donald Sullivan, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Sonia Wong, #11288.

Gabriel Y. Cho, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, JianJun Xie.
No appearance by defaulted defendant(s) Vicente Navarro.
Default of Defendant(s) has been duly entered.
Plaintiff Jianjun Xie sworn and examinded on his own behalf through Mandarin Interpreter Danto Su.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant(s) as follows:
